subject: Ecology
title: Rachel Carson
description: A quick, practical guide to Rachel Carson.

hook.text: In 1962, Rachel Carson published 'Silent Spring' and changed how the world sees pesticides.

buildup.text: She documented how DDT accumulated through food chains, killing birds at the top.

discovery.text: Her work proved that chemicals don't stay where you spray them—they move through webs.

twist.text: The chemical industry attacked her fiercely, but the science held. DDT was banned.

climax.text: Carson's work sparked the modern environmental movement and the creation of the EPA.

quiz.question: What did 'Silent Spring' expose?
options: How DDT moved through food chains and killed wildlife
